channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Gosh, a Club Where Bosch’s Little Creatures Might Go for Cocktails
item.description: Gosh, a new nightclub in Lower Manhattan by way of Italy, has its own quirky logic.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Schade and Marissa Alper
item.media:description: Alivia Allers and Matthew Hutchinson on one of Gosh’s plush couches upstairs.

item.title: Trump’s D.C. Renovations, HGTV Style
item.description: President Trump brings a particular brand of reality TV-style renovations to the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: After generations of slow and often staid improvements, the Trump era has already added a splashy flair to today’s Washington, including an Ultimate Fighting Championship octagon cage rising on the South Lawn, ready to host a fight to celebrate the nation’s 250th birthday.

item.title: These NYC Reservations Are Impossible to Get. Except During a Knicks Game.
item.description: Some New Yorkers tried their luck at hot spots like Bong, Via Carota and Ha’s Snack Bar.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Graham Dickie
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times

item.title: They Tracked Fitness, Food and Sleep Obsessively. Now They’re Tuning Out.
item.description: As some online lean into biohacking and wellness metrics, reformed optimizers are canceling Pilates and having that extra glass of wine.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy
item.media:credit: Gili Benita for The New York Times
item.media:description: There are more ways than ever to track your health. Some have found themselves a little too obsessed with the stats.

item.title: At the Tribeca Festival Artists Dinner, Celebrities Cheered on the Knicks
item.description: Actors cheered on the Knicks at Chanel’s annual Tribeca Festival Artists Dinner, which became something of an unofficial celebrity watch party.
item.dc:creator: Sophia June and Krista Schlueter
item.media:description: Rashida Jones, left, and Rosie Perez.

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Wedding Has Users Placing Bets on Polymarket and Kalshi
item.description: Call it “parasociality exploded” or an invasion of privacy, but that hasn’t stopped bettors on prediction market sites.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Jason Miller/Getty Images
item.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are engaged. The two have said little about their wedding publicly, but some online bettors are convinced they can predict where and when the two will marry.

item.title: The Pro-Tax Socialite
item.description: Julie Macklowe’s unlikely crusade.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein
item.media:credit: Eric Helgas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Julie Macklowe argued that New Yorkers who are “wealthy in precisely the way I am” shouldn’t avoid New York taxes.

item.title: Can These ChatGPT Ads Make You Love A.I.?
item.description: ChatGPT goes with heartwarming retro vibes to sell a product that has become a source of concern for most Americans.
item.dc:creator: Steven Kurutz
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: Billboards for ChatGPT, part of a new ad campaign, rise above a corner tavern in the Greenwich Village neighborhood of Manhattan.

item.title: How to Eat Pizza with a Male Model
item.description: Order slices, fold them in half, flirt with male model, hope it makes your boyfriend jealous.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Krischer
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: A Teen Summer Romance and the Journal He Kept for Her
item.description: When Danit Hetsroni and Joshua Raikin met at summer camp, both had already navigated challenges that forced them to grow up quickly.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Katz

item.title: A Veterinarian, a Life Coach and a Highly Persuasive Pair of Donkeys
item.description: Dr. Evelyn Ivey knew Thomas Rotelli had the qualities she wanted in a mate, but watching how animals gravitated to him clinched it.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Katz

item.title: Matthew MacNabb and Laura Nolan of ‘Dancing with the Stars Ireland’ Marry
item.description: Laura Nolan developed a crush on Matthew MacNabb when he was on “Love Island UK.” Then they became dance partners on another show.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: ‘Darling, I Love You, but Give Me Park Avenue’
item.description: Lexie Riegelhaupt and Gabe Greenberg sang the “Green Acres” theme song as she left Park Avenue for his home in Carroll Gardens, Brooklyn.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: A Celebration and a ‘Love Ritual’ Shaped by Their Community
item.description: At the wedding of Danny Mefford and Yazmany Arboleda, guests made various contributions, including decorating the church and forming a choir.
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: The Four Suits You Should Have in Your Closet
item.description: Suits are back, and no matter the season or occasion, these styles are guaranteed to carry you through.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Montgomery

item.title: William Coupon, Who Wanted to ‘Photograph Everyone in the World,’ Dies at 73
item.description: Self-taught, he became a go-to portraitist for politicians, rock stars and other celebrities. He also documented Indigenous people and inmates on death row.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

item.title: What Does ‘Triggered’ Really Mean?
item.description: This popular term is often misused, experts say, which may cause more harm than good.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Wenner Moyer

item.title: Running Around N.Y.C. With ‘Deli Boys’ Mob Boss Auntie
item.description: Poorna Jagannathan, who plays Lucky Auntie in “Deli Boys” on Hulu, is finally over the impostor syndrome.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta
item.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The actress Poorna Jagannathan, 53, last month in New York.

item.title: Mike Amiri Knows What Travis Kelce and Marcus Rashford Want to Wear
item.description: How Amiri built a brand on the culture of sports.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Ricardo Nagaoka for The New York Times

item.title: Taylor Swift and Haim Sisters Appear at MSG for Knicks Game
item.description: The musician, who is believed to be planning a wedding in the city, showed up in a musical take on Knicks gear.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
item.media:description: Taylor Swift and Este and Alana Haim came to support the Knicks.

item.title: Graham Platner Tucked in His Shirt on Primary Night
item.description: On election night, the Democratic candidate from Maine wore a button-up, a deviation from the candidate’s unvarnished image.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner, and his tucked-in dress shirt, on Tuesday night.

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Joy to Your Day
item.description: Dancing pigeons, mystery stew and a nostalgia machine.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch
item.media:credit: Illustration by Mike McQuade. Pigeon: Library of Congress

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘The “Right” Answers in Life’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: ‘Backrooms,’ Liminal Spaces and Gen Z Anxiety
item.description: “Backrooms,” a horror movie about an uncanny labyrinthine space, plays on a generation’s fears about where digital life and the real world collide.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: A24, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The new horror movie out from A24 is the feature-length version of a viral YouTube video.

item.title: Diljeet Dosanjh Taylor, B.Y.U. Track Coach, Won’t Change for Her ‘Haters’
item.description: Diljeet Dosanjh Taylor, an outspoken feminist in Mormon country, has helped make Brigham Young University into a running powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Scott Cacciola and Spenser Heaps
item.media:description: Coach Diljeet Dosanjh Taylor watches her runners during a recent practice.

item.title: My Sister-in-Law Made an Indecent Suggestion After a Night of Drinking. Help!
item.description: A reader is unsure whether to tell his wife about her sister’s drunken musings, including an imagined scenario in which they might rekindle their long-ago love affair.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes
item.media:credit: Miguel Porlan

item.title: For the NBA Finals, These Knicks Fans Show Out in Style
item.description: Fans want to support their team, but they’re not willing to squelch their sense of style.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha and Jacob Gallagher

item.title: The Joy of Shopping in Los Angeles
item.description: Men, they’re not afraid to spend! Plus, have we hit Knicks merch overload?
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: Artemis Astronauts to Wear Prada Spacesuits
item.description: Prada and Axiom Space unveiled the bodysuit that will go to the moon.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lorenzo Bertelli, right, Prada’s chief marketing officer and head of sustainability, onstage with Axiom Space officials to unveil the new Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment at Prada’s store in the SoHo neighborhood of Manhattan.

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Pinstripes From London
item.description: Statement trousers were judged to be worth the splurge in a London boutique.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: How Much Nudity Does Instagram Allow? Butt Magazine Would Like to Know
item.description: The only thing consistent about the social media platform’s rules on showing naked body parts appears to be its inconsistency in enforcing them.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein
item.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; Getty Images
item.media:description: Content creators who have been suspended are puzzling over Meta’s community standards.

item.title: Unforgettable Looks at the Tony Awards: Cole Escola, Sarah Paulson and More
item.description: Shrunken suits, bouncy wigs and gloves that came with a manicure were some of the looks that stood out on the red carpet.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cole Escola in Christopher John Rogers at the 79th Tony Awards.

item.title: At the Tony Awards After-Parties, a ‘Harry Potter’ Reunion and Winner Celebrations
item.description: A crowd that included Daniel Radcliffe, Cole Escola and Carrie Coon celebrated with whiskey ice cream at Rockefeller Center and show tunes at the Carlyle hotel.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr, Nancy Coleman, Nina Westervelt and Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet
item.category: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child (Play)
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
item.media:description: Megan Thee Stallion dancing to a song performed by Solea Pfeiffer at the Tony Awards after-party at the Carlyle hotel in Manhattan on Sunday.

item.title: While New York Celebrates the Knicks’ Finals Appearance, Some Residents Shrug
item.description: Not everyone in the city is being swept up in the team’s championship drive. Some are just trying to see a Broadway show, or spin some yarn.
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia
item.media:credit: DeSean McClinton-Holland for The New York Times
item.media:description: It might not seem like it, given all the jerseys one sees on the street these days. But it is a provable fact that not every New Yorker is all in on the Knicks.

item.title: What’s the Best Way to Wear a Crop Top?
item.description: A reader wonders whether they are for teenagers only.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Jacopo Raule/Getty Images

item.title: Tony Awards 2026 Red Carpet: See the Looks of Broadway’s Biggest Stars
item.description: Here’s what Broadway’s biggest names wore to walk the blue carpet at the 79th Tony Awards.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis/Reuters
item.media:description: Jeremy Pope, in Robert Wun, making an entrance that can only be described as “theatrical.”

item.title: Bruce Springsteen Talks About His Place in American Music
item.description: As he opens an expansive cultural center not far from the Jersey Shore boardwalk, the singer describes himself as “a small link in a big chain.”
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.category: Springsteen, Bruce, Archives and Center for American Music
item.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bruce Springsteen outside the new Bruce Springsteen Center for American Music.

item.title: My Boss Is a Manipulative Creep. What Can I Do?
item.description: Plus: How much unpaid work can a recruiter demand? And what on earth is that smell?
item.dc:creator: Max Read
item.media:credit: Margeaux Walter for The New York Times

item.title: Zegna Reimagines the Malibu Beach Bum
item.description: The Italian men’s wear label relocates its range of soft tailoring and nubby knits to coastal California.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: No Longer ‘Supporting Cast,’ Grooms Now Want Glam Squads, Too
item.description: The wedding industry is seeing an emerging ecosystem of services for grooms, from day-of hair and makeup to concierges and even therapists.
item.dc:creator: Perri Ormont Blumberg

item.title: The Niche Designer Dressing the A-List in Scraps
item.description: ‘It’ girls are lining up for Giovanna Flores scraps.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale
item.media:credit: Tessa Belle Dillman for The New York Times

item.title: Bruce Springsteen Opens His Music Center
item.description: Jon Bon Jovi, Public Enemy and others joined him to ring in the Bruce Springsteen Center for American Music, a $50 million archive and cultural hub.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.category: Springsteen, Bruce, Archives and Center for American Music
item.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bruce Springsteen greets Jon Bon Jovi at a ceremony for the Bruce Springsteen Center for American Music in Long Branch, N.J., on Saturday.

item.title: Celebrities Love Bike Dates. Should You Try One, Too?
item.description: Celebrities have recently been hitting the bike lane with their beaus, but the “Bicycle Boy” was once branded a type to be avoided.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus
item.media:credit: Lawrence Schwartzwald/Sygma, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A 1995 “Sex and the City” column warning of the pitfalls of dating a “Bicycle Boy” granted a technical exception to John F. Kennedy Jr., “New York’s most famous and sought-after bike-riding bachelor.”

item.title: People Love to Hate This Fake Private Equity Guy
item.description: Johnny Hilbrant Partridge has found social media fame as PE Guy, a character who embodies obnoxious entitlement.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay

item.title: Hermès Brings Parisian Flair to Bel Air
item.description: The French fashion house stages its first women’s show in Los Angeles, with a lot of leather and some West Coast glam.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.category: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An Hermès show with a view.
